annotations.label: Surface as two-dimensional manifold

anchor.snippet: a surface is a two-dimensional manifold

annotations.note: Mathlib formalizes general manifolds via `IsManifold`/`ChartedSpace` but has no dedicated two-dimensional surface notion.

annotations.label: Klein bottle not embeddable in 3-space

anchor.snippet: the Klein bottle is a surface that cannot be embedded in three-dimensional Euclidean space

annotations.note: The Klein bottle is not defined anywhere in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Surface as deformed plane

anchor.snippet: a surface is a geometrical shape that resembles a deformed plane

annotations.note: This is an informal intuitive description with no Mathlib counterpart.

annotations.label: Surface as two-dimensional space

anchor.snippet: A surface is a two-dimensional space

annotations.note: Informal lead description; the precise topological-manifold version is captured by `ChartedSpace` (see the topological-surface entry).

annotations.label: Topological surface

anchor.snippet: A (topological) surface is a topological space in which every point has an open neighbourhood

annotations.note: A topological manifold is a `ChartedSpace` over a model space, but the 2-dimensional (surface) specialization is not separately defined.

annotations.label: Coordinate chart

anchor.snippet: together with the corresponding homeomorphism, is known as a (coordinate) chart

annotations.note: Charts are formalized as atlas members / `chartAt`, homeomorphisms onto open subsets of a general model space.

annotations.label: Surface with boundary

anchor.snippet: a (topological) surface with boundary is a Hausdorff topological space in which every point has an open neighbourhood

annotations.note: Manifolds-with-boundary are supported via `ModelWithCorners`/Euclidean half-space models, but not a 2D surface-with-boundary notion.

annotations.label: Boundary point

anchor.snippet: A point on the surface mapped via a chart to the x -axis is termed a boundary point

annotations.note: `ModelWithCorners.IsBoundaryPoint` defines boundary points for general manifolds with corners.

annotations.label: Interior point

anchor.snippet: a point mapped to above the x -axis is an interior point

annotations.note: `ModelWithCorners.IsInteriorPoint` defines interior points for general manifolds with corners.

annotations.label: Closed disk as surface with boundary

anchor.snippet: The closed disk is a simple example of a surface with boundary

annotations.note: Manifold-with-boundary infrastructure exists, but the closed disk is not given such a structure in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Closed surface

anchor.snippet: A surface with empty boundary which is compact is known as a 'closed' surface

annotations.note: Compactness plus `BoundarylessManifold` capture this, but there is no dedicated closed-surface (2D) notion.

annotations.label: Examples of closed surfaces

anchor.snippet: The two-dimensional sphere, the two-dimensional torus , and the real projective plane are examples of closed surfaces

annotations.note: The sphere has a (compact) manifold instance, but the torus and real projective plane are not formalized as surfaces.

annotations.label: Orientable surface

anchor.snippet: a surface is said to be orientable if it does not contain a homeomorphic copy of the Möbius strip

annotations.note: Manifold orientability (and the Möbius strip) is not defined in Mathlib; only vector-space orientation exists.

annotations.label: Extrinsic surface

anchor.snippet: as such was termed extrinsic

annotations.note: Mathlib has no notion of an extrinsically/embedded-defined surface.

annotations.label: Intrinsic surface

anchor.snippet: which is the definition that mathematicians use at present, is intrinsic

annotations.note: The intrinsic/abstract manifold definition corresponds to `ChartedSpace`, though not specialized to surfaces.

annotations.label: Whitney embedding theorem

anchor.snippet: the Whitney embedding theorem asserts every surface can in fact be embedded homeomorphically into Euclidean space

annotations.note: Mathlib proves a Whitney embedding theorem for compact smooth manifolds of any dimension into Euclidean space.

annotations.label: Embedding compact surfaces in E^3

anchor.snippet: any compact surface that is either orientable or has a boundary can be embedded in E 3

annotations.note: This surface-specific embedding result into E³ is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Steiner surfaces

anchor.snippet: Steiner surfaces , including Boy's surface , the Roman surface and the cross-cap , are models of the real projective plane in E 3

annotations.note: Steiner/Boy/Roman surfaces are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Alexander horned sphere

anchor.snippet: The Alexander horned sphere is a well-known pathological embedding of the two-sphere into the three-sphere

annotations.note: The Alexander horned sphere is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Homeomorphic but non-isotopic tori

anchor.snippet: The two embedded tori are homeomorphic, but not isotopic

annotations.note: Isotopy of embeddings and embedded tori are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Parametric surface

anchor.snippet: is said to be a parametric surface

annotations.note: No notion of parametric surface exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Implicit surface

anchor.snippet: the locus of zeros of f does define a surface, known as an implicit surface

annotations.note: No notion of an implicit surface (zero locus as a manifold) is formalized.

annotations.label: Construction from fundamental polygon

anchor.snippet: Each closed surface can be constructed from an oriented polygon with an even number of sides, called a fundamental polygon of the surface

annotations.note: Fundamental polygons and surface construction from them are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Fundamental group presentation from polygon

anchor.snippet: turns out to be the sole relation in a presentation of the fundamental group of the surface

annotations.note: The fundamental-group presentation of a surface from its polygon is not formalized.

annotations.label: Projective plane as quotient of sphere

anchor.snippet: the real projective plane can be obtained as the quotient of the sphere by identifying all pairs of opposite points on the sphere

annotations.note: The real projective plane as a quotient of the sphere is not formalized as a surface in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Connected sum

anchor.snippet: The connected sum of two surfaces M and N , denoted M # N , is obtained by removing a disk from each of them

annotations.note: The connected sum of surfaces/manifolds is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Euler characteristic of connected sum

anchor.snippet: is the sum of the Euler characteristics of the summands, minus two

annotations.note: Neither the connected sum nor the Euler characteristic of surfaces is formalized.

annotations.label: Sphere is identity for connected sum

anchor.snippet: The sphere S is an identity element for the connected sum

annotations.note: The connected sum operation is not formalized, so this identity property is absent.

annotations.label: Associativity of connected sum

anchor.snippet: The connected sum is associative

annotations.note: The connected sum operation is not formalized, so its associativity is absent.

annotations.label: P # P is the Klein bottle

anchor.snippet: The connected sum of two real projective planes, P # P , is the Klein bottle K

annotations.note: Connected sums, projective planes, and the Klein bottle are all unformalized.

annotations.label: Connected sum with projective plane is nonorientable

anchor.snippet: Any connected sum involving a real projective plane is nonorientable

annotations.note: Connected sums and surface orientability are not formalized.

annotations.label: Closed surface

anchor.snippet: A closed surface is a surface that is compact and without boundary

annotations.note: Compactness plus `BoundarylessManifold` express this, but there is no dedicated closed-surface (2D) notion.

annotations.label: Examples of closed surfaces

anchor.snippet: Examples of closed surfaces include the sphere , the torus and the Klein bottle

annotations.note: The sphere has a manifold instance, but the torus and Klein bottle are not formalized as surfaces.

annotations.label: Closed iff boundary of a solid

anchor.snippet: A surface embedded in three-dimensional space is closed if and only if it is the boundary of a solid

annotations.note: This characterization of closed embedded surfaces is not formalized.

annotations.label: Classification theorem of closed surfaces

anchor.snippet: The classification theorem of closed surfaces states that any connected closed surface is homeomorphic to some member of one of these three families

annotations.note: The classification of closed surfaces is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Genus of a surface

anchor.snippet: The number g of tori involved is called the genus of the surface

annotations.note: The genus of a surface is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Euler characteristic of connected sum of g tori

anchor.snippet: the Euler characteristic of the connected sum of g tori is 2 − 2 g

annotations.note: The Euler characteristic of surfaces and connected sums is not formalized.

annotations.label: Euler characteristic of connected sum of projective planes

anchor.snippet: the Euler characteristic of the connected sum of k of them is 2 − k

annotations.note: The Euler characteristic of surfaces and connected sums is not formalized.

annotations.label: Closed surfaces classified by Euler characteristic and orientability

anchor.snippet: a closed surface is determined, up to homeomorphism, by two pieces of information

annotations.note: The surface classification by Euler characteristic and orientability is not formalized.

annotations.label: Monoid of closed surfaces under connected sum

anchor.snippet: the closed surfaces up to homeomorphism form a commutative monoid under the operation of connected sum

annotations.note: The monoid of closed surfaces under connected sum is not formalized.

annotations.label: Dyck's theorem

anchor.snippet: This relation is sometimes known as Dyck's theorem after Walther von Dyck

annotations.note: Dyck's theorem on connected sums of surfaces is not formalized.

annotations.label: Compact 2-manifolds are triangulable

anchor.snippet: every compact 2-manifold is homeomorphic to a simplicial complex

annotations.note: Mathlib has simplicial complexes but no triangulation theorem for compact 2-manifolds.

annotations.label: Compact surfaces with boundary as closed surfaces with holes

anchor.snippet: Compact surfaces with boundary are closed surfaces with a finite number of holes

annotations.note: This structural result about surfaces with boundary is not formalized.

annotations.label: Classification of compact surfaces with boundary

anchor.snippet: A compact connected surface with boundary is classified by the orientability and Euler characteristic of the corresponding closed surface and the number of boundary components

annotations.note: The classification of compact surfaces with boundary is not formalized.

annotations.label: Genus of compact surface with boundary

anchor.snippet: The genus of a compact surface is defined as the genus of the corresponding closed surface

annotations.note: Surface genus is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Jacob's ladder and Loch Ness monster

anchor.snippet: two canonical counterexamples are the Jacob's ladder and the Loch Ness monster

annotations.note: These non-compact surface examples are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Space of ends of a non-compact surface

anchor.snippet: A non-compact surface M has a non-empty space of ends E ( M )

annotations.note: The topological space of ends is not formalized (only graph ends and categorical ends exist).

annotations.label: Space of ends is a closed subspace of the Cantor set

anchor.snippet: The space E ( M ) is always topologically equivalent to a closed subspace of the Cantor set

annotations.note: Although `cantorSet` exists, the space of ends and this embedding result are not formalized.

annotations.label: Classification of non-compact surfaces with finite handles/planes

anchor.snippet: classify the surface M up to topological equivalence

annotations.note: The classification of non-compact surfaces is not formalized.

annotations.label: Long line product, non-second-countable surface

anchor.snippet: the Cartesian product of the long line with the space of real numbers

annotations.note: The long line is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Prüfer manifold

anchor.snippet: is the Prüfer manifold , which can be described by simple equations that show it to be a real-analytic surface

annotations.note: The Prüfer manifold is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Radó's theorem

anchor.snippet: Tibor Radó proved that all Riemann surfaces

annotations.note: Radó's theorem on triangulability of Riemann surfaces is not formalized.

annotations.label: Polyhedra as surfaces

anchor.snippet: Polyhedra , such as the boundary of a cube , are among the first surfaces encountered in geometry

annotations.note: Polyhedron boundaries are not formalized as surfaces in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Smooth surface

anchor.snippet: It is also possible to define smooth surfaces , in which each point has a neighborhood diffeomorphic to some open set in E 2

annotations.note: Smooth manifolds (`IsManifold I ∞ M`) are formalized generally, not specialized to 2D surfaces.

annotations.label: Smooth surfaces diffeomorphic iff homeomorphic

anchor.snippet: Two smooth surfaces are diffeomorphic if and only if they are homeomorphic

annotations.note: This smoothing/equivalence result for surfaces is not formalized.

annotations.label: Gauss–Bonnet theorem

anchor.snippet: the famous Gauss–Bonnet theorem for closed surfaces states that the integral of the Gaussian curvature K over the entire surface S is determined by the Euler characteristic

annotations.note: The Gauss–Bonnet theorem appears only as a target in docs/1000.yaml, not formalized.

annotations.label: Riemann surface

anchor.snippet: A complex one-manifold is a smooth oriented surface, also called a Riemann surface

annotations.note: Complex manifolds exist as `IsManifold` over ℂ, but there is no dedicated Riemann surface (1-dim complex manifold) definition.

annotations.label: Compact orientable surfaces as Riemann surfaces

anchor.snippet: every compact orientable surface is realizable as a Riemann surface

annotations.note: Realization of compact orientable surfaces as Riemann surfaces is not formalized.

annotations.label: Uniformization theorem

anchor.snippet: One version of the uniformization theorem (due to Poincaré ) states that any Riemannian metric on an oriented, closed surface is conformally equivalent to an essentially unique metric of constant curvature

annotations.note: The uniformization theorem appears only as a target in docs/1000.yaml, not formalized.

annotations.label: Complex surface

anchor.snippet: A complex surface is a complex two-manifold and thus a real four-manifold

annotations.note: Complex manifolds are formalized as `IsManifold` over ℂ; the specific 2-complex-dimensional surface notion isn't separately defined.
